item.title: It’s urchin that you look at the single most important invention of all time
item.description:
m’urchin, you’re looking sharp but I think that’s the point
I’m sea urchin for more puns, it’s an emergency.
#aquarium #cute_animal #seaurchin edit - Please don't do this at home or in the ocean with PLA! While PLA is much better than other plastics, it can still turn into microplastics so it's best not to put it in water. A sauce: https://www.3dnatives.com/en/pla-filament-230720194/

item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Cosplay: Pro Photos VS Selfies
item.description:
SnarkyJayCosplay here, with something I felt was interesting and worth sharing— the value of good photography when it comes to cosplay! I’ve been cosplaying for six years, and for the first 3-4 of that, felt completely uncomfortable in front of the camera. As I’ve tried to improve upon my craft, I’ve had to get used to being in front of the camera and collaborating with the person behind the lens, not just being afraid of them.
My pops is the guy behind all my pro-looking photoshoots. On weekends, we set up in our living room to shoot: a roll of grey paper and four flashes. The flashes go as follows: one hair-light above me, one side light to highlight and separate me from the background a bit more (creating the blue tone you can see on screen-left), a light to fill me in on my left side (screen right) because I like to be photographed from the right and you always photograph the darker side, and a light that hits the background with color!
As you can see, there’s a HUGE difference between these two photos (not just the bodysuit). The one on the left is with a single ring light in a bedroom. I’m standing right in front of it, my phone on a timer: the result is grainy quality and flat lighting. On the right, we’ve got the setup I described previously, plus a far better quality camera (a Canon R5). You can see the skin tones are far more accurate, the background is less distracting, the photo quality is smooth, and there’s actually separation and definition in areas like my jaw, cheekbone, etc.
Of course, shooting with a photographer does have its challenges. Without my phone screen in front of me, I can’t see exactly what the poses I’m doing look like. Additionally, there’s another human’s performance to rely on: is the photo in focus, are they snapping as quickly as I’m moving, etc. I’m really lucky to work with my dad, who I love and trust, has always been supremely supportive of my craft, and is a master with a camera in his hands— so even though these factors do come into play, we work really well together.
All in all, there’s no shame in self-shot content! I know the ONLY reason I’m able to produce content of the caliber on the right is because I’m lucky to have my dad’s talent, gear, and cooperation on my side. Being that photographers (on or off the con floor) aren’t cheap, I can completely understand the need for self-shoots. However— I think it’s easy for folks to look at photography service and equipment prices and dismiss it as overpriced: so I wanted to show the value of having it. Thanks for reading!
item.dc:creator: rectalpossum

item.description: Please keep this in mind that some people can't be around it. I currently am fighting a migraine triggered by someone smoking it next to my car. I'm aware of my weak constitution. item.title: Mugshots of the Patriot Front dorks
item.description:
You may have seen pictures of the 31 Best Buy Nazis getting arrested over the weekend, and as usual they had their masks on for much of it. Fun thing about being arrested is you get your picture taken, like Splash Mountain, but free, and there are no masks allowed. https://www.spokesman.com/stories/2022/jun/12/sheriffs-office-releases-names-of-31-patriot-front/
Alexander N. Sisenstein (Alex didn’t fit in the grid so he gets to go first)
Branden M. Haney
Cameron K. Pruitt
Colton M. Brown
Connor P. Moran
Conor J. Ryan
Dakota R. Tabler
Derek J. Smith
Devin W. Center
Dylan C. Corio
Forrest C. Rankin
Garret J. Garland
Graham J. Whitsom
James J. Johnson
James M. Johnson
Jared M. Boyce
Josiah D. Buster
Justin M. Oleary
Kieran P. Morris
Lawrence A. Norman
Mishael J. Buster
Mitchell F. Wagner
Nathan D. Brenner
Nathaniel T. Whitfield
Richard J. Jessop
Robert B. Whitted
Spencer T. Simpson
Steven D. Tucker
Thomas R. Rousseau
Wesley E. Van Horn
Winston W. Durham
And that’s your lot. What a bunch of clowns. https://www.npr.org/2022/06/11/1104405804/patriot-front-white-supremacist-arrested-near-idaho-pride?t=1655119507603

item.title: Jerome Horwitz, CURLY in "THE THREE STOOGES"
item.description: Jerome Horwitz, famous for playing CURLY in "THE THREE STOOGES", was known to all as a protector of dogs. Curly's contract with Columbia Pictures included a clause that allowed his dogs to accompany him on the studio lot. Columbia limited it to no more than two dogs at a time, this due to the puppies' unplanned on-camera appearances from time to time. You can still see those surprise dog on set invasions in the first few short films. Typically surrounded by various dogs, Curly was known to come home with a stray dog ​​and foster it until he could find it a permanent home. When the Stooges were out on the road, Curly took it upon himself to find a new home for at least one stray dog ​​in every town they visited. Curly is estimated to have saved and rescued more than 5,000 dogs in his lifetime. This makes him a man ahead of his time, with a very admirable concern for man's best friend. Dogs are family ❤
item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: I love how this story ends.
item.description: Earlier today, I posted something that made me question whether the meanings of symbols/words could be taken back, when terrible people use them to spread hate. This little boy seeing "Let's Go Brandon" flags, and thinking they were meant to encourage him, shows that it might just be possible. Gives me a glimmer of hope, anyway. Biden sending him a letter is the icing on the cake. This genuinely warmed my heart.
item.dc:creator: ButtersTheCat

item.title: Hidden Room (I did it again)
item.description:
Completed office build, can ya see the hidden door? Scroll down and follow along! *EDIT* Video: https://i.imgur.com/RPDKtJw.mp4
Looking into the office after I added a center wall to split the big open area
Both basement wells have locking metal covers to prevent unwanted intruders.
4 gang electrical box
Cat5e for the RFID keypad
New light switch for the room behind the office
Drywall done
TV wall drywall done
Oops, hole in the wrong piece…
Fake electrical junction box for the RFID keypad on left
Mud n tape
Dog supervising the build #dogtax
Making some progress
Texture
Black walls where the wood will go (to hide cracks between wood)
Blue walls cuz why not?
All the pallet wood bundled up so it was easy to carry down to the basement. These were burned with a propane weed burner.
Separating by size
First row was the hardest to get perfectly level (technically the 2nd row was first)
Carpet padding going in
Carpet
Notice bottom row of wood not yet in place till after carpet was finished.
The hidden door with drywall and laying up the boards
The door and wood overlap to hide the seam.
Sugatsune large hinges were used, they are AMAZING.
Can’t even tell the door is there, bottom row of wood in place.
What door?
Ready for lights and TV
Wall Sconce
Almost done
Fake electrical junction box plate to hide the RFID keypad held on by magnets
Finished
Movie night
item.dc:creator: ThatKTMguy

item.title: And we see yet another method of terrifying children.
